title: Fluffy Bitter Moroccan Chickpeas Tagine
description: A light, airy chickpea tagine infused with the sharp bite of mustard seeds and fenugreek, perfect for a dairy‑free, energizing dinner.

ingredients.name: Mixed aromatics (onion, garlic, ginger)
ingredients.amount: 1 medium onion diced, 3 cloves minced, 1 inch grated ginger

ingredients.name: Spices (smoked paprika, ground cumin, ground turmeric)
ingredients.amount: 1 tsp smoked paprika, 1 tsp ground cumin, 1 tsp ground turmeric

ingredients.name: Mustard seeds
ingredients.amount: 1 tbsp, soaked in 2 tbsp warm water 10 min then drained

instructions: Soak the mustard seeds: place in a bowl with 2 tbsp warm water, let stand 10 minutes, then drain and set aside.
Heat olive oil in a heavy‑bottom pot or tagine over medium heat.
Add the mixed aromatics (onion, garlic, ginger) and sauté until the onion becomes translucent and fragrant, about 4 minutes.
Stir in the spices (smoked paprika, cumin, turmeric) and toast for 1 minute to release their oils.
Add the drained chickpeas, 2 cups of water, and lemon juice to the pot; bring to a gentle boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, uncovered, for 10 minutes to meld the flavors.
While the chickpeas simmer, whisk the chickpea flour with 1 cup of water until smooth to form a thin batter.
Pour the batter into the pot, stirring constantly until the chickpeas are lightly coated and the mixture thickens slightly; this creates a fluffy, airy coating.
Simmer for another 5 minutes, then stir in the fenugreek leaves and fresh herbs; season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the tagine hot, optionally accompanied by a side of couscous or crusty bread.
